sn11.5:0.3: Victory Through What is Well Spoken
sn11.5:1.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery. There he addressed the monks, “Monks!” “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him. The Blessed One said,
sn11.5:1.2: “Once in the past the devas & asuras were arrayed for battle.
sn11.5:1.3: Then Vepacitti the asura-king said to Sakka the deva-king:
sn11.5:1.4: ‘Let there be victory through what is well spoken.’
sn11.5:1.5: “‘Yes, Vepacitti, let there be victory through what is well spoken.’
sn11.5:1.6: “So the devas & asuras appointed a panel of judges, (thinking,)
sn11.5:1.7: ‘These will decide for us what is well spoken & poorly spoken.’
sn11.5:1.8: “Then Vepacitti the asura-king said to Sakka the deva-king,
sn11.5:1.10: “When this was said, Sakka the deva-king said to Vepacitti the asura-king,
sn11.5:1.11: ‘But you are the senior deity here, Vepacitti.
sn11.5:1.13: “When this was said, Vepacitti recited this verse:
sn11.5:2.4: should restrain the fool with a heavy stick.’
sn11.5:3.1: “When Vepacitti had said this verse, the asuras applauded but the devas were silent.
sn11.5:3.4: “When this was said, Sakka recited this verse:
sn11.5:4.2: is the only constraint for a fool:
sn11.5:5.1: “When Sakka had said this verse, the devas applauded but the asuras were silent.
sn11.5:5.4: “When this was said, Vepacitti recited this verse:
sn11.5:6.4: “He’s forbearing out of fear of me,”
sn11.5:6.5: the idiot pursues you even more—
sn11.5:6.6: as a bull, someone who runs away.’
sn11.5:7.1: “When Vepacitti had said this verse, the asuras applauded but the devas were silent.
sn11.5:7.4: “When this was said, Sakka recited this verse: ‘It doesn’t matter whether he thinks, “He’s forbearing out of fear of me.”
sn11.5:8.1: One’s own true good is the foremost good.
sn11.5:9.4: They call that strength no strength at all: whoever’s strength
sn11.5:10.1: is the strength of a fool.
sn11.5:10.3: for one who is strong, guarding—guarded by—Dhamma.
sn11.5:10.4: You make things worse when you flare up
sn11.5:11.4: wins a battle hard to win.
sn11.5:12.1: You live for the good of both
sn11.5:13.1: When you work the cure of both
sn11.5:13.3: those who think you a fool
sn11.5:14.1: “When Sakka had said this verse, the devas applauded but the asuras were silent.
sn11.5:14.2: Then the deva & asura panel of judges said,
sn11.5:14.3: ‘The verses said by Vepacitti the asura-king
sn11.5:14.4: lie in the sphere of swords & weapons—thence arguments, quarrels, & strife.
sn11.5:14.5: Whereas the verses said by Sakka the deva-king
sn11.5:14.6: lie outside the sphere of swords & weapons—thence no arguments, no quarrels, no strife.
sn11.5:14.7: The victory through what is well spoken goes to Sakka the deva-king.’
sn11.5:14.8: “And that, monks, is how the victory through what was well spoken went to Sakka the deva-king.”
